State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance     Administration  • • •  Executive/Legal  • • •  Enforcement  • • •  Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901            322-6441         322-6444

May 15, 1980


Ms. Cindy Martin, Bookkeeper
United Public Employees Local 390
522 Grand Avenue
Oakland, CA 94610

Dear Ms. Martin:

This letter will confirm advice given to you by me on the tele-
phone concerning your Local's obligation to file campaign state-
ments under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.

The Fair Political Practices Commission's Regulation 18419 allows
for all of the political activity of your local which would
otherwise be reportable under the law to be included on the
campaign statements filed by the Service Employees International
Union.

I would suggest that you forward a copy of the regulation (I have
enclosed a copy for your convenience) to the Washington D.C.
office so they may know exactly what is required to be reported
on their statements.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Political Reform Consultant II

Enclosure: 2 FPPC Cal. Admin. Code 18419

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                          Date : April 23, 1980




From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Regina Siciliano-Kutchins

Subject: United Public Employees Local 390 -
         Correspondence dated 4/16/80 re. 2 Cal.
         Admin. Code 18419


         I sent Cindy Martin of UPE Local 390 a copy of
regulation 18419 and advised her that if the activity
of her local was covered by the conditions included
in the regulation they need not report as a committee
under the law.

FPPC

APR 13 4 28 PM '80


April 16, 1980


Ms. Cindy Martin, Bookkeeper
United Public Employees Local 390
522 Grand Avenue
Oakland, CA 94610

Dear Ms. Martin:

Thank you for your letter of April 7, 1980, requesting
this office to confirm advice provided by Ms. Regina
Siciliano-Kutchins of the Fair Political Practices Com-
mission.

Only the Commission is empowered by the Political
Reform Act of 1974 to issue opinions or written advice
with respect to a person's or organization's duties under
that law. If you require written confirmation of Ms.
Siciliano-Kutchins' advice, I suggest you request it of
her directly.

Sincerely,


DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
Political Reform Division

cc: Ms. Regina Siciliano-Kutchins ←

DBP:tf

United Public Employees Local 390

Affiliated with the Service Employees International Union, the largest AFL-CIO Union in California

PAUL VARACALLI
Executive Secretary

522 Grand Avenue
Oakland, Calif. 94610

Phone 465-0120

April 7, 1980

RECEIVED
AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

APR 0 9 1980

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

Office of the Secretary of State
Political Reform Division
P O Box 1467
Sacramento, Ca. 95807

Dear Sirs:

Per the advice of Regina Hutchins of the
the Political Reform Commission based on
regulation #18419 this committee is not required
to file form 420 in connection with the attached
referenced contribution. If you have any questions
regarding this matter please let me know as soon
as possible, if not please send me confirmation
as to Ms Hutchins ruling.

Representing over
6,000 public employees

Alameda County

Walnut Creek

Sincerely,

Cindy Martin
Bookkeeper

Mr John Sweeney
General Secretary-Treasurer
Service Employees Intl Union AFL CIO
2020 K St N W
Washington D C 20006

Re  ANTI-PROPOSITION 9 CONTRIBUTION

Dear John

Pursuant to our conversation yesterday, please transit
a Check in the amount of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000)
from our Strike Fund Account to the California SEIU
State Council for use in the Anti-Proposition 9 campaign.

Additionally, I would appreciate it if you would transfer
One Thousand Dollars ($1,000) from our Political Fund
to the State Council so it could be transmitted to the
California Tax Reform Association for use in the Tax
Simplicity Act campaign.

Thank you for your continuing assistance.

Sincerely & fraternally

Paul Varacalli

cc  G Hardy
    T Twomey

upc# 390-aflcio

202 452-8750

SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION
AFL-CIO, CLC

2020 K Street, N.W.,                                    Washington, D.C. 20006

George Hardy                                            John J. Sweeney
International President                                 International Secretary-Treasurer


                                    March 19, 1980




Mr. Timothy J. Twomey
Secretary-Treasurer
California State Council, SEIU
240 Golden Gate Avenue
San Francisco, California 94102

Dear Sir and Brother:

Enclosed are two checks, one in the amount of $5,000.00 and
the other in the amount of $1,000.00 which were requested
by Local 390. These checks were drawn from money Local 390
has in their strike fund and PEA money and are for use in
the Anti-Proposition 9 campaign.

                                    Fraternally yours,


                                    [signature]
                                    John J. Sweeney
                                    Secretary-Treasurer

April 28, 1980

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Ms. Siciliano-Kutchins:

Enclosed you will find copies of all correspondence
regarding monies contributed in our name to the
California SEIU State Council -Anti-Proposition
9 campaign.

This contribution was sent from Washington
directly to the State Council and has never passed
through our books, therefore I have no information as
to the actual disbursement of the monies. The State
Council does file with the Political Reform Division
when it receives monies in our name since they have
all the information regarding disbursements.

In a conversation with you on April 7, 1980 I under-
stood that we did not have to file under regulation
#1419. I sent a letter to that effect to the
Political Reform Division and was told that I could
only get a written confirmation from your office.
Please review the enclosed letters and let me know
if we indeed are exempt from filing the 420.

Sincerely,

Cindy Martin
Bookkeeper
